description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray surface speckled with numerous small, rounded, lighter-colored inclusions, giving it a distinctive spotted appearance. The rock's texture appears rough and uneven, with some areas showing more pronounced bumps and pits. One side of the rock is lighter in color, possibly due to weathering or a natural fracture, while the other sides are darker and more densely speckled. There is a small, rectangular label affixed to one face, bearing handwritten text, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The rock's overall form is blocky but asymmetrical, with no flat surfaces large enough to serve as a seat or platform. Its visual characteristics suggest it could be a type of conglomerate or breccia, commonly found in urban landscaping or as a natural obstacle in parks.
description: A rough, gray rock with dense, light-colored speckles and an irregular, blocky shape, featuring a small label on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's most speckled and detailed face, with a visible label attached near the bottom.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a lighter, less speckled surface, possibly a fractured or weathered side.
description_view_2: This side displays a long, flat face densely covered in small, round inclusions, emphasizing the rock's spotted texture.
description_view_3: This view presents a darker, more rounded surface with a uniform distribution of speckles and no visible label.
